title: Transit advocates call the Orange Line extension proposal ‘not very serious.’ Two Boston councilors say it’s overdue.
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Local News While a proposed Orange Line extension could take 15 to 20 years to finish, the bus project is a more “near-term improvement,” a transit advocate said.
Boston City Councilors Miniard Culpepper, left, and Brian Worrell, right, speak to the media after pitching a proposal to extend the Orange Line.
Craig F.

title: 1 injured in road rage incident involving gun in South Shore library parking lot
author: Audrey Cooney
bullets: Local News A passenger in one of the cars involved fired a gun during a road rage incident, the Hingham Police Department said.
Someone fired a gun during a road rage incident outside a library in Hingham on Thursday afternoon, police said.
Police responded to the parking lot of the Hingham Public Library at 4:22 p.

title: DA: Man charged with murder allegedly killed friend by mistake during gunfight in Dorchester
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Crime Prosecutors say the Dorchester man shot his friend “in the confusion of the gunfight.
” A man is facing a murder charge after he allegedly shot and killed his friend during a Dorchester gunfight in May, prosecutors said.

Justin Ferrer, 23, of Dorchester, pleaded not guilty and was ordered held without bail during his arraignment in Boston Municipal Court’s Dorchester division on Thursday, Suffolk District Attorney Kevin Hayden’s office said in a press release.

title: After body work, my car needs to be recalibrated
author: John Paul
bullets: How To John Paul, AAA Northeast's Car Doctor, hears from a reader who was told their car needs a recalibration after undergoing body work.
The Car Doctor hears from a reader who was told their car needs a recalibration after undergoing body work.
Justin Sullivan/Getty Images By John Paul July 23, 2026 | 6:07 PM 4 minutes to read ShareSend this article to your social connections.

title: Measures to deter wrong-way driving put into motion by state trooper’s death are now law
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Local News The new law requires MassDOT to develop a statewide strategy to prevent wrong-way crashes through roadway improvements, technology, education, and annual reporting.
State troopers stand at attention during Trooper Kevin Trainor's funeral service outside of St.
James Church in Salem on May 13, 2026.

title: Lindsay Clancy trial: Opening statements slated for Monday as attorneys clash over sensitive evidence
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Crime “What I can’t fathom is the district attorney’s intent to put a circus in front of this jury that’s calculated to just inflame the passions of the jury.
” Lindsay Clancy confers with her lawyer, Kevin Reddington, on July 22, 2026.
Greg Derr/The Patriot Ledger, Pool By Abby Patkin July 23, 2026 | 4:47 PM 3 minutes to read ShareSend this article to your social connections.

Kyle Williams catches a pass during OTAs in 2025.

title: Mass. Democratic Party Chair Steve Kerrigan charged with drunk driving last month
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Crime Kerrigan was pulled over late at night in Southborough, according to a police report.
Massachusetts Democratic Party Chair Steve Kerrigan speaks at the party's convention in Worcester in May.

John Tlumacki/Boston Globe Steve Kerrigan, the chair of the Massachusetts Democratic Party, was charged with drunk driving after being pulled over in Southborough last month, according to court documents.

title: Former Lynn educator, one of Amazon’s ‘teachers of the year,’ charged with child rape
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Crime Prosecutors allege Tymond Tran sexually abused a then-14-year-old student while teaching the eighth grade in 2022 to 2023.

By Abby Patkin July 23, 2026 | 2:22 PM 2 minutes to read A former Lynn educator who was once hailed as one of Amazon’s “teachers of the year” has been charged with sexually abusing a 14-year-old middle schooler.

Tymond Tran, 38, pleaded not guilty Wednesday to charges of aggravated child rape, indecent assault and battery on a person 14 or older, exhibiting a child in a nude or lascivious pose, and distributing obscene matter to a minor.

title: After medical emergency, Boston Councilor Mejia says she does not have ‘heart condition’
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Politics Councilor Julia Mejia was told to wear a heart monitor as a precaution but continued to attribute her medical episode to dehydration and exhaustion.
Councilor Julia Mejia in April.
Lane Turner/Boston Globe After collapsing during a meeting earlier this month, Boston City Councilor Julia Mejia returned to the council chambers this week and provided more clarity on her health.
